summary: Fire‑filled Fillmore show with scorching Smokestack and soaring Dark Star
blurb: The set bursts open with a blistering Smokestack Lightning duel that carries through a high‑energy first set. The second half peaks with an expansive Dark Star, the Stephen / Not Fade Away sandwich, and a triumphant Turn On Your Lovelight finale. Overall performance is strong despite some mix balance quirks.
review: On February 8, 1970 at the Fillmore West the Dead delivered a high‑octane set that still feels fresh decades later. Pigpen’s organ roars on the opening Smokestack Lightning while Jerry and Bob trade scorching guitar lines, creating one of those rare duels that fans talk about for years. The first set rolls through rootsy staples—Morning Dew, China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider—and a solid sit‑down with Sitting On Top Of The World before the band dives into an exploratory Dark Star in the second set. That celestial journey segues into a legendary Saint Stephen > Not Fade Away > Saint Stephen sandwich and crescendos with a 35‑minute Turn On Your Lovelight that carries the night to a triumphant close. While the matrix/audience mixes are not flawless—drums sit low in the mix and early audience bleed is audible—the overall energy and musicianship make this show a must‑listen for anyone chasing that early‑70s Fillmore magic.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All three recordings note strong highlights (Smokestack Lightning duel, Dark Star soaring, Stephen/Not Fade Away sandwich) and consistently praise the band's interplay, while also mentioning mix balance issues. The overall tone across analyses is mixed‑to‑positive, warranting a modest positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Matrix mix with good soundboard sections, captures the fire‑filled performance while minimizing tape hiss and drop‑outs.

key_highlights: Blistering Smokestack Lightning opener featuring a rare guitar duel.
Soaring Dark Star that showcases Garcia’s lead work and expansive improvisation.
Legendary Saint Stephen > Not Fade Away > Saint Stephen sandwich.
Epic Turn On Your Lovelight closing the show with sustained energy.

band_performance.Jerry: Garcia’s lead work shines—crisp tone on Smokestack, soaring leads in Dark Star, and high‑part harmonies on China Cat Sunflower; his duel with Bob during Smokestack is a show‑stealing moment.
band_performance.Bob: Weir’s vocal/guitar interplay praised throughout, especially during the Smokestack duel and second‑set jams; noted for screaming vocals on the return to Saint Stephen.
